title: Prime Palindrome
description:
Given an integer n, return _the smallest prime palindrome greater than or equal to_ n.

An integer is prime if it has exactly two divisors: 1 and itself. Note that 1 is not a prime number.

*   For example, 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, and 13 are all primes.

An integer is a palindrome if it reads the same from left to right as it does from right to left.

*   For example, 101 and 12321 are palindromes.

The test cases are generated so that the answer always exists and is in the range [2, 2 * 108].

solutionCode.python:
```python
def isRectangleOverlap(rec1, rec2):
    return rec1[2] > rec2[0] and rec1[3] > rec2[1] and rec1[0] < rec2[2] and rec1[1] < rec2[3]
```
    
    The algorithm checks if the rectangles overlap by verifying that the `x` coordinate of the top-right corner of `rec1` is greater than the `x` coordinate of the bottom-left corner of `rec2`, the `y` coordinate of the top-right corner of `rec1` is greater than the `y` coordinate of the bottom-left corner of `rec2`, the `x` coordinate of the bottom-left corner of `rec1` is less than the `x` coordinate of the top-right corner of `rec2`, and the `y` coordinate of the bottom-left corner of `rec1` is less than the `y` coordinate of the top-right corner of `rec2`. If all these conditions hold, it means that the rectangles overlap, otherwise they do not.
